steps: CO bets 40 into 80 on the flop and BTN has 9 clean outs.
The call costs 40 to win 120, so the price is 33%.
The rule of 4 gives about 36% equity, so BTN calls.
Key takeaway: compare the price to the draw before acting.
lesson_goal: Goal: Core Pot Odds Equity Demo 001.
reaction_text: The call is supported by the math.

steps: UTG bets 60 into 120 on the turn and MP has 8 clean outs.
The call costs 60 to win 180, so the price is 33%.
The rule of 2 gives about 16% equity, so MP folds.
Key takeaway: turn draws often lose value fast when the price is high.
lesson_goal: Goal: Core Pot Odds Equity Demo 002.
reaction_text: The fold protects chips when the numbers are bad.

steps: BTN bets 20 into 60 on the flop and SB has a gutshot plus two overcards.
The direct price is close, and future betting can add value when the draw improves.
SB calls with a plan to continue only on strong turn cards.
Key takeaway: close spots depend on both immediate odds and future value.
lesson_goal: Goal: Core Pot Odds Equity Demo 003.
reaction_text: A close spot can be playable with enough future upside.
